calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-03-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bacon for and with (executor) Thelma Taylor. Annette Shelton (estate of Hermana Grant) in Propria Persona. Larry Saxxon (beneficiary) in Propria Persona. Mary Mayo for the James Tisher estate. Paul Cheatham, II (beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Bacon stated that Ms. Shelton's claim against the estate was settled for $15,000.00 and that she no longer has a claim. Mr. Bacon reported that mediation with Betty Epstein was complete, explained calculations regarding interest, that there was less in the estate than the will distributed and everyone had agreed to reductions and agreed that the sisters should receive their full amount and that the order for distribution have been amended to incorporate the language in the will for the Black Nurses Association. Mr. Saxxon stated that he participated in the mediation, that the amounts he calculates are different that what Mr. Bacon and the probate referee calculates, what they agreed to in mediation is not what they signed, and that Mr. Bacon's extraordinary fees and Ms. Shelton's lawsuit have used up a substantial amount of the money. Mr. Cheatham stated that he was present for the mediation and that everyone signed off on what was decided. Mr. Bacon addressed the issues in the examiner's notes, explaining that part 10e was a further explanation of the voluntary reduction, that the extraordinary fees were a result of issues regarding the wire transfer at Bank of America, the lawsuit brought forth by Ms. Shelton and fees paid for the mediator, accountants, filings and postage. Mr. Bacon also stated that no objections have been filed. Mildred Crear of the Bay Area Black Nurses Association was introduced.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. Court Repoter: Rhiannon Smith, CSR # 13265, 619-318-1225. (Reported)

Petition To Confirm Mediation Settlement Agreement For Final Distribution, First And Final Account And Report Of Executor, For Ordinary Compensation To Executor, For Ordinary Attorney Fees And Costs And For Extraordinary Attorney Fees And Costs

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-18-2014 Continued To Aug-13-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bacon for and with Thelma Taylor. Annette Shelton Executor for the Estate of Hermana Grant in Propria Persona. Larry Saxton Beneficiary in Propria Persona. Ofelia Long and Mildred Crear (on the boards of the bay area black nurses associations) also present.

Discussion was held with regard to Mr. Bacon's interpretation of the Will, the request to reduce the Black Nurses Association's share to $300,000.00, abatement of certain gifts, the request to reduce Vicky's share for legal fees, Ms. Shelton's position that Ms. Grant is entitled to more than $15,000.00, alternative interpretation of the Will/distribution and the request for extraordinary fees. Ms. Long indicated that the black nurses associations are not in agreement with a reduction. Mr. Bacon stated that he does not see a need to for a reserve. The accounting petition was continued to August 13, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Ms. Shelton to file her objections, for Mr. Bacon to file a response, for the Executor to file a petition for interpretation of the Will and disregarding of the second p.s. clause, and a petition concerning payment by Vicky of fees.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Court Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR#13654 (415) 981-3498. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-04-2012 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Continued To Sep-04-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Continued To Aug-21-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Bacon for Victorine Houston and for and with Thelma Taylor. Jason Wolford Creditor (related civil case 507657 Grant v Freeman-McGuire). Mary Wiley (Sister of Decedent) in Propria Persona. Anita Jones (Sister of Decedent) in Propria Persona. Annetta Shelton Administrator of the Estate of Hermana J. Grant in Propria Persona. Ophelia Long and Mildred Crear from Bay Area Black Nurses and National Black Nurses Association.

Ms. Wiley expressed her concerns with regard to Fiduciary neglect, conflict of interest of Mr. Bacon, removal of personal items, items being sold with out Executor Letters, release of the bank account, the $1,000,000.00 check, etc. Mr. Bacon expalined that Mr. Murray from the Bank filed an interpleader in Napa because he believes that although the account shows Ms. Wiley as the Beneficiary, the proceeds from the sale of the 18th Street property came in after Michael's death and that they belong to the Estate. The Court suggested appointment of a neutral Special Administrator to address payment of the storage facility, payment of the trailer in Yountville, and all of the other items that need to be dealt with soon and asked the Parties to discuss the matter outside of the courtroom. Upon returning, all Parties were present except for Ms. Long and Ms. Crear. Mr. Bacon stated that Ms. Wiley and Ms. Jones agreed to drop their objections with the sole appointment of Thelma Taylor and to provide information (adresses, ages, names of Parents) on all of the 16 Nephews and Nieces. The Court advised Counsel that a declination to serve as Executor from Victorine Houston was needed. The Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary was continued to August 21, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Bacon to file an amended petition for appointment of Ms. Taylor only with the same hearing date of August 21, 2012, the declination of Ms. Houston and provide notice to George McGuire, Leola (last name unknown), and the Nephews and Nieces. The letters received from Ms. Wiley and Ms. Jones were returned to them in open Court. Ms. Wiley and Ms. Jones were advised that anything they wanted the Court to consider needed to be filed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR#13654. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
